title: dysoxylum cupuliforme
intro: Dysoxylum cupuliforme, also known as the Malabar or Indian rosewood, is a tropical tree species native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The extracts of Dysoxylum cupuliforme have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, providing health benefits like pain relief and wound healing. Dysoxylum cupuliforme is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are made by drying the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant and then mixing them with other herbs to create medicinal remedies.
